events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a column/beam erection & plumb operation on an industrial site. The key issue is that the view cuts off the area where exposure control would be visible, and the crew location and energy/fall-protection boundary cannot be determined from the provided frame.

Since critical safety information (fall protection, crew location, energy boundary) cannot be determined from the observation, the appropriate decision is "needs_more_info" rather than "fail" or "pass." We cannot confirm whether the work is safe or unsafe because the critical safety controls are not visible.

Let me construct the JSON response.

```json
    "Obtain a full view of the work zone including fall-protection boundaries and crew positions before continuing",
    "Verify energy isolation and control zones are established per OSHA 1926 Subpart R",

    "Do not proceed with further erection until exposure controls can be visually confirmed"
  ],
  "rationale": "The observation frame cuts off the critical safety-control area. Without confirming fall protection, crew location relative to the load, and energy isolation boundaries, the erection cannot be verified as safe or compliant. Active column/beam erection is inherently high-risk under Subpart R, so inability to confirm controls warrants needs_more_info rather than pass.",

    "AISC 303",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart R"
  ]
}
```
